evals.prompt:
I have this HTML for our app's login page and need to write automation for it in Python using Playwright. Can you generate a page object model? ```html

Sign In

Invalid credentials. Please try again.
Forgot your password?
```
evals.expected_output: A Python class (LoginPage) using Playwright sync API with locator properties for all elements (email, password, remember-me checkbox, error message, forgot-password link), action methods (enter_email, enter_password, click_login, click_forgot_password), and state/verification methods (get_error_message, is_error_visible). Optionally a BasePage class. No test assertions inside the POM.

assertions.text: Output uses Python syntax (class definition, def, self, snake_case method names)
Output uses Playwright Python API (page, Locator, get_by_label / get_by_role / locator methods)
Defines a class named LoginPage (or similar descriptive name)
Covers all 5 key elements: email input, password input, remember-me checkbox, error message, forgot-password link
Has named action methods for filling email, filling password, and clicking the login button
Has at least one state/verification method that returns text or boolean (e.g., get_error_message, is_error_visible)
No test assertions (assert statements) inside the POM class itself

evals.prompt: I want to create a TypeScript + Playwright page object model for the login page at https://www.saucedemo.com. We prefer data-testid attributes for selectors.
evals.expected_output: A TypeScript class (LoginPage) using Playwright's TypeScript API with readonly Locator fields, using getByTestId or data-testid CSS selectors for the saucedemo login page elements (username input, password input, login button, error container). Action methods enterUsername, enterPassword, clickLogin. At least one verification method. Should reflect actual saucedemo DOM (data-test attributes like 'user-name', 'password', 'login-button', 'error-button-container').

assertions.text: Output uses TypeScript syntax (type annotations, readonly, async/await, export class)
Output uses Playwright TypeScript API (Page, Locator, getByTestId or locator with data-testid)
Defines a class named LoginPage (or similar)
Uses data-testid / getByTestId selectors for at least the primary interactive elements
Covers username input, password input, and login button (the core saucedemo login elements)
Has at least one verification/state method (e.g., getErrorMessage, isErrorVisible)
No test assertions inside the POM class

evals.expected_output: A Java class (CheckoutPage or CheckoutFormPage) using Playwright Java API with Locator fields defined using getByLabel for aria-labeled inputs. Should cover all 6 inputs (first name, last name, email, card number, expiry, cvv), error summary, back button, and place order button. Methods named after user actions (enterFirstName, enterLastName, etc., clickPlaceOrder, clickBackToCart). Verification method for error state. BasePage optional.

assertions.text: Output uses Java syntax (class, public/private, return types, PascalCase class name)
Output uses Playwright Java API (Page, Locator, page.getByLabel / page.getByRole / page.locator)
Defines a Java class with a descriptive name ending in 'Page' (e.g., CheckoutPage, CheckoutFormPage)
Uses getByLabel() or getByRole() for the aria-labeled inputs rather than CSS/XPath
Covers all 6 form fields: first name, last name, email, card number, expiry, CVV
Covers both buttons (back to cart, place order) and the error summary element
Has named action methods for filling form fields and clicking both buttons
No test assertions inside the POM class
